weather: Today in Edmonds, WA: Partly cloudy skies becoming overcast by afternoon. High 61°F, low 42°F. Light NNW winds shifting SSE at 5-10 mph. No precipitation expected—just a typical cool spring day with comfortable humidity. Evening stays dry with clearing after midnight.

global_news.findings: Suspect charged with attempted assassination of Trump at Washington dinner: Cole Tomas Allen, 31, from California has been charged with attempting to assassinate President Trump during the White House Correspondents' Dinner. The incident occurred when shots were fired inside the Washington Hilton hotel, prompting Secret Service agents to rush Trump to safety. The suspect allegedly wanted to kill as many high-level officials as possible.

Germany commits to massive military expansion amid European uncertainty: German President Frank-Walter Steinmeier announced Germany will step up its NATO contribution with a 500 billion euro ($569 billion) special fund for infrastructure and defense, marking a historic shift from post-war restraint. This comes as Europe faces "epochal geopolitical shifts" with Putin's war in Ukraine and uncertainty over US commitment to European defense.

Mass shooting at Nigerian football pitch kills at least 29: Gunmen attacked a football field in north-east Nigeria, killing at least 29 people according to local governor reports. The attack represents escalating violence in the region, with armed groups increasingly targeting civilian gatherings.

Hezbollah-Israel tensions escalate with cross-border strikes: Hezbollah and Israel exchanged threats and military strikes across Lebanon's border, raising fears of broader regional conflict. The escalation comes amid ongoing US-Iran tensions and follows recent Israeli operations in southern Lebanon.

UK tightens asylum rules to exclude foreign sex offenders: Britain will deny refugee protections to foreign nationals with sex offense convictions under new Border Security, Asylum and Immigration Bill amendments. The government will also introduce AI-assisted asylum decision-making to speed up processing of the 90,686 pending cases.
